SBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DECEMBER 29, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTIONS 36(A), (D) & (E). AFTER MATTER WAS HEARD AND ARGUED, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULINGS AS FOLLOWS: THE PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE IS APRIL 25, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. DISCOVERY WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL THE TRIAL DATE. SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE FILED ON 15 DAYS NOTICE, WITH NO ADDITIONAL DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS APRIL 14, 2011. PLAINTIFFS MUST MAKE A DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS BY APRIL 7, 2011. MSC WILL BE HELD ON APRIL 14, 2011 AT 1PM IN ROOM 603. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING TO SCHEDULING OR CASE MANAGEMENT ISSUES. PATHOLOGY MATERIALS FROM MRS. NICHOLS ARE TO BE PROVIDED TO THE DEFENDANTS FOR REVIEW WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF THE COURT'S ORDER, AND TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SCHEDULE FOR REVIEW. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: S. RAVINANTAPRICHA. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (220)

DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

RULING:
Prior to the hearing, defendant General Electric Company made an ex parte application to set aside the Court's order regarding preference granted in Dec. 2010. After discussion, counsel withdrew the application and requested the Court to continue the trial date instead. Argued and the Court granted defendant General Electric's motion to continue trial. The trial is ordered continued to May 9, 2011 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 220. Discovery remains open as to issues of the treating physician, a CT scan and possible further IME only.
Defendant General Electric Company's motion for summary judgment was argued and ordered continued to May 4, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 220. Plaintiff's objection to the continuance was heard and overruled. Defendant's supplemental brief is due by 4/27, plaintiff's supplemental opposition by 5/2, defendant's reply by 5/3. No tentative ruling will be issued. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Marc Brainich, Esq., of SEDGWICK DETERT MORAN & ARNOLD LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party General Electric Company
Rett D. Bergmark, Esq., of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Luisa Nichols, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR RELIEF PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 473 AND TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORY RESPONSES
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant General Electric Company's motion for relief pursuant to C.C.P. 473 and to compel further interrogatory responses is granted. Defendant showed that its failure to file its motion to compel within the 45-day period was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, namely, plaintiffs' counsel's representation that plaintiffs would provide substantive responses as soon as defendant provided a date certain for the deposition of its PMK. Plaintiffs must provide substantive verified responses to defendant's special interrogatories within five days. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Taking Of Deposition Of Defendant 3m Companys Pmk
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Continued To Mar-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mordecai D. Boone, Esq., of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party 3M Company
Rett D. Bergmark, Esq., of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Luisa Nichols, et al.

RULING:
The Court and counsel discussed the status. Plaintiffs' motion to compel the taking of depositions of 3M Company's production at deposition of its person most knowledgeable, custodian of records and requested documents is continued to March 10. Pursuant to CCP 639(a)(5) and CRC 3.920(a), appointment of a discovery referee is warranted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a referee. If the parties cannot agree, each side (plaintiffs being one side and all defendants remaining in the case being one side) must submit the names of three nominees to all parties and the court (by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org) by Wednesday March 9 at 5:00pm. The parties will then have until the hearing at 9:00am on Thursday March 10 to object to the nominees. Each side may object to up to two nominees from the other side. Each nominee should be able to immediately start resolving discovery disputes. This appointment applies to all motions regarding any defendant's person most knowledgeable deposition and related document production, as well as any other issues the parties agree to submit to the referee, and any other motions or application later added by court order. Defendants may proceed either through Berry & Berry or on their own. This tentative ruling will apply to all motions filed by plaintiffs regarding defendant's PMK deposition that are set to be heard on March 9 and 10. Plaintiffs' counsel must provide a copy of this tentative ruling to all remaining defendants in this action. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions To Written Discovery, To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC's motion to deem requests for admission admitted, to compel responses to written discovery and for sanction is denied in full. Plaintiffs established sufficient factual basis for relief from their failure to serve responses when required by the code. Defendant's failure to make any meet and confer effort prior to filing the motion, while not required for an order to compel, precludes an award of sanctions in that it cannot be said that the motion was 'necessitated' by plaintiffs' inaction. Had defendant made any meet and confer effort, the motion would likely not have had to be filed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
